headline: Apple At 50 Weighs AI Partnerships Satellite Ties And Succession
summary: Apple (NasdaqGS:AAPL) marks its 50th anniversary as investors focus on CEO Tim Cook's eventual succession and the company's long term direction. Apple plans to open Siri to third party AI assistants on iOS, signaling a shift toward a more open approach to digital assistants. Amazon is reported to be in talks to acquire Globalstar, Apple's satellite partner for emergency and connectivity features. Apple enters this milestone moment with a current share price of $255.92 and multi year returns...

headline: SpaceX Is Worth $2 Trillion? What That Might Mean.
summary: SpaceX is worth how much?  Bloomberg reported Thursday that SpaceX was seeking an initial public offering valuation of about $2 trillion, up from earlier estimates of $1.8 trillion.  SpaceX was valued at $1.25 trillion after its merger in February with xAI.

headline: Big Tech H-1B Filings Plunge As Amazon, Google, Meta And Microsoft Slash Jobs Amid Rising Immigration Costs
summary: H-1B visa applications at major tech companies fell sharply in late 2025 as layoffs rose and stricter U.S. immigration rules increased costs and scrutiny. Big Tech H-1B Applications Drop Federal data shows Amazon.com Inc.), Alphabet Inc.'s Google, Meta Platforms, Inc., and Microsoft Corp filed significantly fewer H-1B petitions in the first quarter of fiscal 2026, as reported by Business Insider on Wednesday. headline: Cloud AI Update - Hidden Costs Surge In Global Enterprise AI Operations
summary: Dan Herbatschek, CEO of Ramsey Theory Group, emphasizes a significant surge in enterprise AI operational costs, which are being underestimated by organizations globally by 30% or more. The costs associated with scaling AI infrastructure are highlighted as a hidden crisis, with enterprises often basing their budgets on pilot projects rather than the extensive demands of full production. Key challenges include the shift from initial model development costs to the ongoing expenses of running AI...

headline: Amazon’s Globalstar play about spectrum, not just hardware
summary: Bloomberg’s Ed Ludlow reported live from Kennedy Space Center that Amazon (NASDAQ:AMZN) is in talks to acquire Globalstar (NASDAQ:GSAT). What Globalstar has is very valuable licensed spectrum. Having a constellation in orbit is not about the hardware up there but about the spectrum that it occupies. — Ed Ludlow, Bloomberg, reporting from Kennedy Space Center ... Amazon’s Globalstar play about spectrum, not just hardware

headline: What markets have learned since Trump's Liberation Day tariffs
summary: This week marks exactly one year since President Trump enacted his "Liberation Day" tariffs last April 2 as the administration took aim at US trading partners and other countries around the world.Yahoo Finance Head of News Myles Udland sits down with Barron's Investor Circle Newsletter editor Josh Schafer, Interactive Brokers chief strategist Steve Sosnick, and StockBrokers.com director of investor research Jessica Inskip to look back on the market's (^DJI, ^IXIC, ^GSPC) reaction to US trade tariff policies and what Wall Street has learned since then.

content.title: What markets have learned since Trump's Liberation Day tariffs
content.description:
This week marks exactly one year since President Trump enacted his "Liberation Day" tariffs last April 2 as the administration took aim at US trading partners and other countries around the world.

Yahoo Finance Head of News Myles Udland sits down with Barron's Investor Circle Newsletter editor Josh Schafer, Interactive Brokers chief strategist Steve Sosnick, and StockBrokers.com director of investor research Jessica Inskip to look back on the market's (^DJI, ^IXIC, ^GSPC) reaction to US trade tariff policies and what Wall Street has learned since then.
content.summary: This week marks exactly one year since President Trump enacted his "Liberation Day" tariffs last April 2 as the administration took aim at US trading partners and other countries around the world.Yahoo Finance Head of News Myles Udland sits down with Barron's Investor Circle Newsletter editor Josh Schafer, Interactive Brokers chief strategist Steve Sosnick, and StockBrokers.com director of investor research Jessica Inskip to look back on the market's (^DJI, ^IXIC, ^GSPC) reaction to US trade tariff policies and what Wall Street has learned since then.

headline: Fidelity’s Active Large-Cap Growth ETF FFLG Is Winning the Battle but Losing the War
